name: Scott and White Employees
description_short: Federally insured state-chartered credit union serving Scott and White Employees in Temple, TX since 1962, with $70.77M in assets and 4,631 members.
description_long: Scott and White Employees Credit Union is a federally insured state-chartered credit union headquartered in Temple, Texas, established in 1962—making it over 60 years old. The institution is headquartered at 2401 S 31st St in Temple and operates a single branch location, serving approximately 4,631 members with a staff of 11 full and part-time employees. As a member-owned cooperative financial institution, it maintains federal insurance protection through the National Credit Union Share Insurance Fund (NCUSIF). The credit union offers a range of traditional banking and financial services including checking and savings accounts, ATM/debit card programs, and loan products. Based on their financial statement, they maintain a diversified loan portfolio consisting of real estate loans ($10.26M), unsecured loans ($2.49M), and other loans ($24.07M), totaling $36.82M in outstanding loans. Their balance sheet shows total assets of $70.77M as of September 2025, with $58.07M in total shares and deposits from members. As a credit union specifically serving Scott and White Employees, this institution maintains a focused membership base tied to employment affiliation, which typically allows for stronger community relationships and member-centric decision-making compared to larger commercial banks. The organization is led by CEO Celeste Kaylor and Chairperson Roy Wohleb. Their most recent audit was completed on February 1, 2025, demonstrating ongoing regulatory compliance and financial transparency. While Scott and White Employees Credit Union provides essential banking services with competitive member-focused benefits, potential members should note that access is restricted to those with employment affiliation with Scott and White, limiting it to a specific eligible population. The single branch location may present convenience limitations for members outside the Temple, Texas area, and the institution does not participate in the Federal Home Loan Bank system, which may affect certain lending capabilities.
pros: Federally insured through NCUSIF, protecting member deposits up to applicable limits
Member-owned cooperative structure prioritizes member interests over shareholder profits
64-year operational history demonstrates stability and longevity in the market
ATM/debit card program available for convenient cash access and payments
Diversified loan portfolio including real estate, unsecured, and other loan products
Recently audited (February 2025) with transparent financial reporting
Low employee-to-member ratio (11 staff for 4,631 members) suggests personalized service potential
cons: Membership restricted to Scott and White Employees, eliminating access for general public
Single branch location limits convenience for members outside Temple, Texas
Significantly smaller asset base ($70.77M) compared to major banks limits product breadth
Not a Federal Home Loan Bank member, potentially restricting certain lending programs
Limited online presence and digital banking information available on their website
best_for: Current and former Scott and White Employees seeking member-owned banking with local service
Members looking for basic checking, savings, and traditional loan products
Individuals who prefer credit union structure and community-focused financial institutions
diagnosis: Scott and White Employees Credit Union is best for existing employees or members of the Scott and White organization seeking traditional banking services from a stable, member-owned financial institution. The primary caveat is membership eligibility—this credit union is restricted to affiliated employees, making it inaccessible to the general public.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
